CREATE PROCEDURE my_schema.my_procedure(IN arg1 INT, IN arg2 VARCHAR(100)) BEGIN SELECT * FROM my_table WHERE column1 = arg1 AND column2 = arg2; END;
CREATE OR REPLACE PROCEDURE my_schema.new_procedure(OUT result INT) BEGIN SELECT COUNT(*) INTO result FROM my_table WHERE column3 IS NOT NULL; END;

CREATE PROCEDURE my_schema.complex_procedure(IN id INT) BEGIN DECLARE new_value INT; SELECT MAX(column1) INTO new_value FROM my_table WHERE id = id; UPDATE my_table SET column1 = new_value WHERE id = id; END;
CREATE PROCEDURE my_schema.join_procedure(IN status VARCHAR(50)) BEGIN SELECT t1.*, t2.* FROM table1 t1 JOIN table2 t2 ON t1.id = t2.foreign_id WHERE t1.status = status; END;

CREATE PROCEDURE my_schema.nested_subquery_procedure(IN threshold INT) BEGIN SELECT * FROM my_table WHERE column1 > (SELECT AVG(column1) FROM my_table WHERE column2 = 'value') AND column3 < threshold; END;
CREATE OR REPLACE PROCEDURE my_schema.data_manipulation_procedure() BEGIN INSERT INTO my_table(column1, column2) SELECT column1, column2 FROM another_table WHERE column3 > 100; END;
CREATE PROCEDURE my_schema.condition_procedure(IN start_date DATE, IN end_date DATE) BEGIN SELECT * FROM events WHERE event_date BETWEEN start_date AND end_date; END;

CREATE PROCEDURE my_schema.cursor_procedure() BEGIN DECLARE done INT DEFAULT 0; DECLARE my_id INT; DECLARE cur CURSOR FOR SELECT id FROM my_table; DECLARE CONTINUE HANDLER FOR NOT FOUND SET done = 1; OPEN cur; read_loop: LOOP FETCH cur INTO my_id; IF done THEN LEAVE read_loop; END IF; -- additional logic here END LOOP; CLOSE cur; END;
